claims.claim: Use kit://clipboard-history?id=<entry_id> as the canonical persisted URI.
claims.counterargument: The URI is resource-shaped and already parsed, but the plan must not imply it is a UI deeplink or image-capable resolver without additional routing/resolution work.

claims.claim: Existing clipboard rows have stable IDs.
claims.counterargument: IDs are stable only while the row exists; add_entry reuses an existing row by content_hash, but a deleted row followed by identical recopy gets a new UUID, leaving old brain links dead.

claims.claim: Retention can be solved by changing prune_old_entries to preserve brain_kept rows.
claims.counterargument: There are multiple deletion paths beyond prune_old_entries, including db_worker prune_impl, clear_unpinned_history, clear_history or clear_impl, remove_entry, and oversized text trimming.
Forward-only only protects new writes; if the user means no raw values anywhere in brain markdown or brain storage, existing raw day lines, fragments, and indexed clipboard docs remain violations.

claims.claim: Add DayEntry::ClipboardRef and persist generic Clipboard entry labels only.
claims.counterargument: The model change is necessary but incomplete unless undo/reject line removal, parser/rendering, and source-label formatting all learn the new line and URI shape.

claims.claim: Stop creating clipboard-origin fragments for long recopy content.
claims.counterargument: This is required for new production writes, but legacy fragments still need read compatibility and optional later migration because they may already contain raw bodies.

claims.claim: Brain search can preserve clipboard searchability by duplicating clipboard content into brain storage.
claims.counterargument: Duplicating raw clipboard content into brain_docs conflicts with strict no-raw-values if brain storage is in scope; search should use clipboard history as a separate provider or accept reduced brain searchability.

claims.claim: Smallest milestone can include all clipboard content types.
claims.counterargument: Image clipboard entries are blob-backed and the current single-entry resource path returns text/plain content; text-only should be the first milestone unless image resource resolution is implemented.

missed_constraints: Retention must enumerate automatic delete paths separately from explicit destructive user actions.
Day Page undo/reject must remove ClipboardRef lines by URI or entry id, not by raw text.
Day Page source labels must map kit://clipboard-history to a generic Clipboard label.
No raw clipboard values means no URL labels, previews, excerpts, fragment bodies, or generated labels derived from clipboard content in brain markdown.
Do not revive post-copy popup UI.
synthesis_must_include: Canonical persisted format should be exactly kit://clipboard-history?id=<entry_id> with no extra preview or label query params.
scriptkit://clipboard/<id> should remain legacy provenance compatibility, not the new canonical persisted markdown URI.

Retention policy should protect brain_kept rows in prune_old_entries, db_worker prune_impl, clear_unpinned_history, and oversized text trimming; explicit remove and clear-all need warning or documented destructive semantics.
Forward-only migration must be described as new-write-only unless an opt-in raw-content migration is added.
Brain indexer raw clipboard duplication is a scope decision that must be resolved explicitly.
Smallest shippable milestone should be text-only ClipboardRef persistence, generic rendering, undo support, and retention protection.
synthesis_must_avoid: Do not claim stable clipboard IDs survive deletion and recopy.
Do not claim prune_old_entries alone prevents dead links.
Do not preserve searchability by copying raw clipboard content into brain markdown or brain_docs without acknowledging the privacy contract violation.
Do not use scriptkit://clipboard/<id> as the canonical new persisted URI.
Do not add source-audit tests where behavior tests can cover the invariant.
Do not include raw URL labels, text previews, excerpts, or note bodies derived from clipboard content.
